name: cntlm
title: cntlm
summary: Fast NTLM authentication proxy with tunneling
description:
Fast NTLM authentication proxy with tunneling
Cntlm is a fast and efficient NTLM proxy, with support for TCP/IP tunneling,
authenticated connection caching, ACLs, proper daemon logging and behaviour
and much more. It has up to ten times faster responses than similar NTLM
proxies, while using by orders or magnitude less RAM and CPU. Manual page
contains detailed information.

commands.name: cntlm
commands.description: authenticating HTTP(S) proxy with TCP/IP tunneling and acceleration

command_examples: cntlm [ -AaBcDdFfGgHhILlMNOPpqRrSsTUuvwXx ] [ host1 port1 | host1:port1 ] ... hostN portN
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use cntlm because Fast NTLM authentication proxy with tunneling.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use cntlm as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cntlm [ -AaBcDdFfGgHhILlMNOPpqRrSsTUuvwXx ] [ host1 port1 | host1:port1 ] ... hostN portN` as documented upstream for cntlm.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair cntlm with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `cntlm --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: cntlm - authenticating HTTP(S) proxy with TCP/IP tunneling and acceleration
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install cntlm. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cntlm [ -AaBcDdFfGgHhILlMNOPpqRrSsTUuvwXx ] [ host1 port1 | host1:port1 ] ... hostN portN`. Fast NTLM authentication proxy with tunneling.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Fast NTLM authentication proxy with tunneling.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: cntlm - authenticating HTTP(S) proxy with TCP/IP tunneling and acceleration.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install cntlm
Install with: sudo apt install cntlm
